learning_outcomes.description: Apply physicochemical principles to the formulation of semi-solid and solid dosage forms and medicines use.
Explain the mechanism of transdermal, pulmonary, and buccal delivery through specialised devices to inform medicines use.
Collate, analyse, and interpret scientific data to inform pharmaceutical decisions.
Perform pharmaceutical calculations relating to semi-solid and solid medicines.
Design and prepare accurate and pharmaceutically elegant extemporaneous semi-solid and solid dosage forms.

assessments.due_date:
Module 1 quiz (15%) 17/08/2026 1:00 pm

Module 2 quiz (10%) 31/08/2026 1:00 pm

Module 3 quiz (10%) 14/09/2026 1:00 pm

Each quiz will be available to complete for 4 days prior to the deadline.
assessments.task_description:
There are three quizzes held during semester:

  • ONE attempt is permitted for each quiz.
  • The quiz questions include the content covered in UQ Extend and the practical class within the relevant module.
  • See the Timetable and Assessment sections on Learn.UQ (Blackboard) for details of each quiz.
  • Each quiz contain a mixture of question types, for example: multiple choice, multiple answer, matching, ordering, fill in the blank/s, jumbled sentence/s, either/or (e.g. true/false), short answer, and short essay style questions.
  • Questions are derived randomly from a pool; therefore, each student may receive a different but equivalent quiz.
  • The quiz will be marked electronically, and correct answers will be awarded the marks indicated beside the question; incorrect answers will be awarded zero.
  • This is an individual assessment that you are required to undertake without any form of communication with other students.
  • As an online quiz undertaken in the venue of your choice, the quiz is open book.
  • This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) technologies, successful completion of this assessment task will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which AI will provide only limited support and guidance.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: The quiz will be completed electronically in the Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
22/10/2026 11:00 am

Held in the scheduled workshop at UQ Dutton Park in Week 12.
assessments.task_description:
General information:

  • This invigilated quiz will be completed in person at UQ Dutton Park during the scheduled workshop in Week 12. 
  • The quiz is paper-based with ONE attempt permitted.
  • The quiz involves various pharmaceutical calculations, mostly related to compounding and dispensing liquid dosage forms, which you will practise in a series of practicals conducted regularly during the semester.
  • The quiz duration is 40 minutes.
  • Questions may be provided from a pool; therefore, each student may receive a different but equivalent quiz.
  • The marks allocated to each question are provided on the paper; answers may be awarded zero, part-marks, or full marks as applicable to each question.
  • This is an individual assessment that will be held under exam conditions.
  • See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) and UQ Extend for more details, a sample/practice quiz, and marking criteria.

Items that may be brought into the quiz:

  • Your Student ID.
  • Calculators must be Casio FX82 series or UQ approved (labelled).
  • Pens: all answers must be written with an indelible pen, not a pencil and not an erasable pen. Mistakes must be neatly crossed through; do not use whiteout.

Items that may NOT be brought into the quiz:

  • Any other items, including any electronic devices.

Materials that will be supplied:

  • The question sheets (including space to provide answers).
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: The Pharmaceutical Calculations In-person Quiz will be completed and submitted during the scheduled workshop at UQ Dutton Park in Week 12. The Deferred Pharmaceutical Calculations In-Person Quiz will be held during the scheduled workshop at UQ Dutton Park in Week 13.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.due_date:
27/10/2026 - 28/10/2026

School-based practical examination, held in the scheduled practical class at UQ Dutton Park in Week 13.
assessments.task_description:
This assessment task is to be completed in person. 

Items that may be brought into the examination:

  • Your Student ID.
  • Laboratory coat, closed-in shoes and safety glasses are mandatory.
  • Calculators must be Casio FX82 series or UQ approved (labelled).
  • Australian Pharmaceutical Formulary (APF) - page tags are allowed, but annotations are not permitted.
  • Pens: all answers must be written with an indelible pen, not a pencil and not an erasable pen. Mistakes must be neatly crossed through; do not use whiteout.

Items that may NOT be brought into the examination:

  • any other items, including any electronic devices.

Materials that will be supplied:

  • All ingredients and equipment required for the product that you will prepare and dispense, the prescription details, a compounding worksheet, and one copy of the current edition of the APF per laboratory.

Instructions:

You will have 90 minutes to complete the exam.

You will work independently to prepare and dispense one assigned prescription. You will complete appropriate calculations, prepare the product, fill out the compounding worksheet, and label the product.

  • Complete the compounding worksheet as you go. Ingredient weights and volumes entered into the compounding worksheet must match the value on the balance or in the measuring cylinder and must be checked by an invigilator. Raise your hand when you need an invigilator to check and sign against your entries.
  • Prepare a suitable label and apply it to the product.
  • To pass, your product must be of acceptable quality to be supplied to a patient. When you consider your product is ready to be supplied, submit the labelled product and compounding worksheet for assessment.
  • Three components are marked, i.e. the product, the label, and the compounding worksheet. A passing grade is achieved when all three components are of acceptable quality for the medication to be dispensed to a patient.
  • See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) and UQ Extend for more details, sample/practice exams, and the marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: The Compounding Skills Assessment will be completed and submitted during the scheduled practical class at UQ Dutton Park in Week 13. Refer to the course Learn.UQ (Blackboard) site for the starting times.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.assessment_title: Semi-solid and solid dosage forms exam
assessments.task_description:
General information:

  • This invigilated in-person digital exam will be completed on your own device (laptop) using the Inspera platform during the end of semester exam period. (as will any deferred or supplementary written exams)
  • Go to the Inspera Assessment Information page, which provides further information on: how to access Inspera, on-campus invigilated exams in Inspera, device requirements, familiarisation opportunities, and submitting assessments.
  • The exam questions will cover the whole course, i.e. Modules 1, 2, 3 and 4.
  • See Learn.UQ (Blackboard) and UQ Extend for more details, a sample/practice exam, and marking criteria.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Submission: Your responses to the questions will be submitted during the exam using your own device (laptop).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 13(27 Jul - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Active online learning via UQ Extend

The content for this course is delivered via UQ Extend. Each, week you should work through the active online learning on UQ Extend. You will get the most out of the weekly workshops if you have viewed the content on UQ Extend before attending the workshop.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Active online learning via UQ Extend The content for this course is delivered via UQ Extend. Each, week you should work through the active online learning on UQ Extend. You will get the most out of the weekly workshops if you have viewed the content on UQ Extend before attending the workshop.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 1 To Week 3(27 Jul - 16 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 1 - Solid dose forms workshops

One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available via Blackboard (Learn.UQ).

Workshops will cover:

  • Week 1) Solid dosage forms, tablets, and quality assessment,
  • Week 2) Particles, powders and tablet formulation,
  • Week 3) Drug dissolution from solid dosage forms.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1 - Solid dose forms workshops One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available via Blackboard (Learn.UQ). Workshops will cover: Week 1) Solid dosage forms, tablets, and quality assessment, Week 2) Particles, powders and tablet formulation, Week 3) Drug dissolution from solid dosage forms.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1 - Solid dose forms practical

A three-hour practical in the laboratories at UQ Dutton Park campus. Working in a team, you will apply pharmaceutical tests to assess tablet quality.

You are required to bring your own lab coat and safety glasses, and to wear closed-in shoes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1 - Solid dose forms practical A three-hour practical in the laboratories at UQ Dutton Park campus. Working in a team, you will apply pharmaceutical tests to assess tablet quality. You are required to bring your own lab coat and safety glasses, and to wear closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 4 To Week 5(17 Aug - 30 Aug)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 2 - Semi-solid dose forms workshops

One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ).

Workshops will cover:

  • Week 4) Semi-solid dosage forms - creams, ointments, pastes, gels,
  • Week 5) Rheology of semi-solids.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2 - Semi-solid dose forms workshops One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ). Workshops will cover: Week 4) Semi-solid dosage forms - creams, ointments, pastes, gels, Week 5) Rheology of semi-solids.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 2 - Semi-solid dose forms practical

A three-hour practical in the laboratories at UQ Dutton Park campus. Working in a team, you will conduct experiments to investigate the formation and viscosity of gels.

You are required to bring your own lab coat and safety glasses, and to wear closed-in shoes.

Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04, L05
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 2 - Semi-solid dose forms practical A three-hour practical in the laboratories at UQ Dutton Park campus. Working in a team, you will conduct experiments to investigate the formation and viscosity of gels. You are required to bring your own lab coat and safety glasses, and to wear closed-in shoes.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 6 To Week 7(31 Aug - 13 Sep)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 3 - Devices workshops

One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ).

Workshops will cover:

  • Week 6) Devices for transdermal delivery,
  • Week 7) Devices for delivery to the mouth and lungs.

Learning outcomes: L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 3 - Devices workshops One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ). Workshops will cover: Week 6) Devices for transdermal delivery, Week 7) Devices for delivery to the mouth and lungs.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 8 To Week 13(14 Sep - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 4 - Compounding workshops

One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ).

Workshops will cover:

  • Week 8) Review compounding creams, prepare for compounding ointments & pastes,
  • Week 9 or 10) Review compounding ointments & pastes, prepare for compounding capsules & suppositories,
  • Week 11) Review compounding process, review pharmaceutical calculations,
  • Week 12) Two-hour workshop: Pharmaceutical calculations in-person quiz followed by workshop review of compounding process,
  • Week 13) Supported revision and exam tips.

Learning outcomes: L04, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 4 - Compounding workshops One-hour weekly workshops held in person at UQ Dutton Park campus. Workshops will be recorded and recordings made available in Blackboard (Learn.UQ). Workshops will cover: Week 8) Review compounding creams, prepare for compounding ointments & pastes, Week 9 or 10) Review compounding ointments & pastes, prepare for compounding capsules & suppositories, Week 11) Review compounding process, review pharmaceutical calculations, Week 12) Two-hour workshop: Pharmaceutical calculations in-person quiz followed by workshop review of compounding process, Week 13) Supported revision and exam tips.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeksFrom Week 8 To Week 13(14 Sep - 01 Nov)
learning_activities.topic:
Module 4 - Compounding practicals

Three-hour weekly practicals in the laboratories at UQ Dutton Park: You are required to bring your own lab coat and safety glasses, and to wear closed-in shoes.

Practicals will cover:

  • Week 8) Creams,
  • Week 9 or 10) Ointments and pastes,
  • Week 11) Suppositories and capsules,
  • Week 12) Mock compounding skills assessment,
  • Week 13) Compounding skills assessment (hurdle exam).

Learning outcomes: L04, L05, L06
